visa_free.notes: All EU and EEA nationals enjoy freedom of movement under EU Treaty rights and are not subject to Schengen short-stay limits. Italy is a Schengen member state. No visa required.

eta.nationality: All Annex II (visa-exempt) third-country nationals (excluding EEA/CH/SM/AND/MC/VAT)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be required for visa-exempt third-country nationals once operational. Timeline: six months after the full implementation of the Entry/Exit System (EES). As of June 2026, no firm launch date has been published by the European Commission. Applies to all Annex II nationals entering the Schengen area including Italy. Fee applies; authorization covers multiple short stays within 3 years.

visa_policy.notes: Italy is a member of the Schengen Area. Visa policy for short stays (up to 90 days in any 180-day period) is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 and applies uniformly across all Schengen states. The regulation is periodically amended. Visa-on-arrival does not exist in the Schengen area. Italy does not offer its own standalone e-visa system for short stays. ETIAS (EU travel authorisation for visa-exempt nationals) is expected to become operational six months after the EES launch, with no confirmed date as of June 2026. All EU/EEA citizens have full freedom of movement. Vanuatu was suspended from Annex II and nationals require a visa; Nauru is listed in Annex II but only subject to a bilateral agreement with the EU that has not yet entered into force — Nauru nationals still require a Schengen visa. Georgia ordinary passport holders remain visa-free; Georgian diplomatic/service/official passport holders subject to EU-wide suspension from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027.

conditional_access.conditions: Lo straniero già residente in uno Stato Schengen e titolare di permesso di soggiorno, è esente da visto per soggiorni non superiori a 90 giorni su un periodo di 180 giorni. [Official Italian MFA: A foreigner already resident in a Schengen State and holder of a residence permit is exempt from visa requirement for stays not exceeding 90 days in a 180-day period.] Must present valid residence permit and valid travel document. Must not be on the Schengen Information System alert list. Must fulfil standard Schengen entry conditions (Article 5(1)(a),(c),(e) of the Schengen Borders Code).

Pursuant to EU Regulation 265/2010, a valid long-stay visa (Type D/national visa) issued by any Schengen member state confers the same free-movement right as a residence permit: the holder may move freely within the territories of other Schengen states for up to 90 days in any 180-day period during the visa's validity. Must fulfil standard Schengen entry conditions and must not be on alert lists. Does not confer automatic right of entry; border authorities may verify entry conditions.

Holders of diplomatic passports from the listed countries do not require a visa to enter Italy for tourism. Stay duration limits and specific agreement dates are not published on the official page. Exemption applies only to national territory of Italy; does not extend automatically to other Schengen states unless covered by a separate bilateral agreement with those states. NOTE: Georgian diplomatic passport holders are subject to an EU-wide suspension of their visa-free access from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 (Georgia was not in this diplomatic list but the EU suspension for Georgian diplomatic passports is noted separately).

Holders of service/official passports from the listed countries do not require a visa to enter Italy for tourism. Stay duration limits and specific agreement dates are not published on the official page. Exemption applies only to national territory of Italy; does not extend automatically to other Schengen states unless covered by a separate bilateral agreement with those states.

Holders of special passports from the listed countries do not require a visa to enter Italy for tourism. Stay duration limits and specific agreement dates are not published on the official page. Exemption applies only to national territory of Italy.

Bilateral agreement between the Government of the Italian Republic and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia on mutual visa exemption for holders of diplomatic, special, or service passports. Signed in Al Ula on 26 January 2025; entered into force on 13 August 2025. Holders may enter and stay in the territory of Italy without a visa for up to 90 days within a six-month period, provided their passport is valid for at least six months. Exemption applies exclusively to the national territory of Italy and does not extend to other Schengen Area countries. Stays exceeding 90 days or diplomatic/official assignments require a visa (application via Verbal Note from Saudi MFA). No visa fee.

EU-wide temporary suspension of visa-free access for Georgian holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports, effective 6 March 2026 until 6 March 2027. This is the first use of the reinforced EU visa suspension mechanism, activated due to Georgia's non-compliance with visa liberalisation commitments in areas of democracy and fundamental rights. Suspension applies uniformly across the Schengen area including Italy. Georgian holders of ordinary biometric passports remain visa-free.

vanuatu.notes: Vanuatu was listed in Annex II of EU Regulation 2018/1806 but the visa-free exemption has been suspended under the EU visa suspension mechanism. The suspension was activated 4 May 2022 due to security risks from Vanuatu's golden passport (CBI) schemes. As of June 2026, the suspension remains in effect and Vanuatu nationals require a Schengen/Italian visa. A Commission proposal to permanently re-introduce the visa requirement for Vanuatu nationals was issued in December 2024; the permanent legislative change may not yet be fully enacted but the suspension makes a visa de facto required.

cbi.notes: Italy does not offer a direct citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Investors who obtain residence through the Investor Visa for Italy program can apply for Italian citizenship only after 10 years of lawful residence in Italy under standard naturalization rules. Dual citizenship is permitted under Italian law. No direct purchase of citizenship exists.

programs.name: Investor Visa for Italy (Italy Golden Visa)
programs.notes: Officially introduced by Law 232/2016 (2017 Budget Law). Administered by the Investor Visa for Italy Committee (IV4I) under MIMIT (Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy). Four investment pathways: (1) EUR 250,000 in an Italian innovative startup; (2) EUR 500,000 in an Italian limited liability company; (3) EUR 1,000,000 as a philanthropic donation to a public interest initiative; (4) EUR 2,000,000 in Italian government bonds. Initial investor visa valid for 2 years. Residence permit valid 2 years, renewable for 3 years. No minimum physical presence requirement. Long-term residence (permanent residence) available after 5 years of maintaining the investment. Path to citizenship via standard naturalization after 10 years of lawful residence. Program suspended for Russian and Belarusian nationals; as of March 2024, suspension extended to non-EU citizens holding dual citizenship with Russian or Belarusian passports. Processing: Nulla Osta issued within 30 days by the Committee; investment must be completed within 3 months of arrival in Italy.

programs.name: Italia Startup Visa (ISV)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU entrepreneurs residing outside Italy who intend to establish an innovative startup in Italy. Business must meet Italian Startup Act criteria: incorporated within 5 years, registered office in Italy, primary activity in development/production/commercialisation of high-tech products/services, R&D expenditure >= 15% of costs/turnover, team of at least 1/3 with PhD or 2/3 with master's degree.
programs.notes: Fully digital, free, bilingual (Italian/English). Issues a self-employment visa valid 1 year, renewable for 2 years. Related program 'Italia Startup Hub' for non-EU nationals already in Italy with valid permits. Applications via portal italiastartupvisa@mise.gov.it.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Italy)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a higher education degree (or post-secondary professional qualification with 3+ years of study, or 5+ years professional experience). Must have a binding employment contract of at least 6 months. Annual gross salary must be at least EUR 33,500 (2024 threshold, between 1x and 1.6x average gross annual salary in Italy).
programs.notes: EU Blue Card issued for 2 years (or duration of employment contract + 3 months if shorter). Quota-exempt. Employer must apply at Single Desk for Immigration (Sportello Unico per l'Immigrazione) at the relevant Prefettura. Italy applies a labour market test before issuing nulla osta. Fee: EUR 50 issuance + EUR 30 postal kit + EUR 16 tax stamp + EUR 30.46 issuance cost.

programs.name: Digital Nomad / Remote Worker Visa
programs.eligibility: Non-EU highly specialised workers who work remotely. Two categories: (1) Digital Nomads: freelancers/self-employed; (2) Remote Workers: employees of foreign companies working entirely remotely. Must demonstrate minimum annual income of EUR 24,789 (as of 2024, calculated as 3x the Italian minimum annual healthcare exemption threshold). Must have valid health insurance (minimum EUR 30,000 coverage). Must hold university degree or equivalent professional qualification relevant to their field.
programs.notes: Officially introduced by Law 25/2022 (Art. 6-quinquies of Legislative Decree 286/1998) and operationalised by Interministerial Decree of 29 February 2024, published in Official Gazette on 4 April 2024. Not subject to annual immigration quotas (decreto flussi). Initial permit valid 1 year, renewable locally at Questura. Spouse and minor children (under 18) can be sponsored once in Italy. Applications at Italian consulates/embassies abroad.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Vanuatu removed from visa_policy.visa_free. The prior record listed Vanuatu in visa_free with a 'suspended' notes field, which is a misclassification — suspended nationals cannot use the visa_free pathway. Vanuatu has been under EU visa suspension since 4 May 2022 due to CBI/golden passport security risks; as of June 2026 the suspension remains in effect and a permanent legislative withdrawal was proposed by the Commission in December 2024. Moved to a visa_required_notable block.

CORRECTION: Georgia entry in visa_free updated to clarify that ORDINARY passport holders remain visa-free, while diplomatic/service/official passport holders are subject to an EU-wide suspension from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027.

ADDITION: New conditional_access entry added for Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport holders (basis=passport_type, level=visa_required) documenting the EU suspension in force from 6 March 2026.
VERIFIED: Diplomatic, service, and special passport exemption lists from conslondra.esteri.it re-confirmed unchanged: 44 diplomatic, 30 service, 7 special countries. Lists match prior record.

VERIFIED: Investor Visa for Italy investment thresholds unchanged: EUR 250,000 startup / EUR 500,000 limited company / EUR 1,000,000 philanthropic / EUR 2,000,000 government bonds. Suspension for Russian/Belarusian nationals confirmed still in place; extended to dual nationals as of March 2024.

VERIFIED: ETIAS still not operational as of June 2026; timeline remains '6 months after EES full implementation' with no confirmed date. ETA entry updated to remove the 'expected 2026' claim from the prior record.
VERIFIED: Saudi Arabia bilateral agreement (signed 26 Jan 2025, in force 13 Aug 2025) confirmed from two esteri.it official sources (ambriad.esteri.it and consgedda.esteri.it). No change needed.
VERIFIED: Kosovo added to Annex II by EU Regulation 2024/1761; source_url updated to the specific amending regulation.

VERIFIED: Nauru appears in Annex II as a conditional entry requiring a bilateral visa exemption agreement with the EU; as that agreement has not entered into force, Nauru nationals are not visa-free and Nauru is correctly absent from visa_free list.
NOTE: The EU adopted its first EU Visa Strategy in January 2026 (COM(2026) 43), but this is a strategic framework document and does not change current Annex I/II country classifications.
gaps: Complete verbatim text of EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II as currently consolidated (including all amendments through 2026) could not be extracted directly from EUR-Lex — the visa-free list was reconstructed from multiple official EU sources.

Vanuatu final legislative status unclear: the Commission proposed permanent removal from Annex II in December 2024, but whether the implementing regulation has been formally adopted and published in the Official Journal as of June 2026 could not be confirmed. The suspension (which has the same practical effect) is confirmed.
Italy does not issue standalone e-visas; no e-visa data to record.
Italy does not offer visa-on-arrival; no VOA data to record.
ETIAS launch date unconfirmed as of June 2026; EES implementation date not published.

Specific salary threshold for EU Blue Card (EUR 33,500) is from the official EC immigration portal for 2024; Italy may update annually — exact 2026 figure unverifiable without accessing the Italian Prefettura/Ministry official gazette.
Digital Nomad Visa minimum income (EUR 24,789) sourced from Italian Consulate General New York (esteri.it) for 2024; annual recalculation not confirmed for 2026.
Diplomatic/service/special passport exemption lists from conslondra.esteri.it do not publish stay duration or bilateral agreement dates for individual countries except Saudi Arabia.
Pakistan-Italy diplomatic passport visa waiver agreement (reported June 2026) has no confirmed official publication on esteri.it; not included pending verification.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption for Italy found on any official Italian or EU government source.
The diplomatic/service/special passport lists from conslondra.esteri.it may not be exhaustive; they represent the published list as accessed June 2026.

EU Regulation 2018/1806 — governing Schengen visa policy Annex I/II. Annex II list confirmed; Nauru noted as conditional (requires bilateral agreement not yet in force).
EU Regulation 265/2010 — Type D long-stay visa equivalence to residence permit for Schengen free movement

EU Commission Implementing Regulation — temporary suspension of visa exemption for Georgian nationals holding diplomatic, service or official passports. Effective 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027.

outbound_official.notes: Italy's Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (MAECI) does not publish a single consolidated outbound visa-free list analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The official authoritative source for Italian citizens traveling abroad is the 'Viaggiare Sicuri' (Travel Safe) portal at www.viaggiaresicuri.it, published by the MAECI Crisis Unit. It provides individual per-country travel sheets (Schede Paese) as PDFs at https://www.viaggiaresicuri.it/schede_paese/pdf/<ISO3>.pdf (e.g. GBR.pdf, USA.pdf). Each sheet contains a 'Requisiti di Ingresso' section with passport, visa, and other entry requirements for Italian citizens. The portal covers all countries. There is no master grouped list; visa categories must be read from each per-country sheet. Examples confirmed from official PDFs: UK — visa-free for tourism up to 6 months (Standard Visitor Visa exemption) but UK ETA mandatory from 2 April 2025 (classified as eta); USA — Visa Waiver Program (VWP) with mandatory ESTA authorization, up to 90 days for tourism/business (classified as eta/evisa since ESTA is a pre-travel electronic authorization). Italy itself is an EU/Schengen state; Italian citizens have full freedom of movement within the EU/EEA and no restrictions within Schengen.
